destinations.evidence: The shared fee crate now calculates audio Phase 2 fees from delivery_result slot 6 outputDurationMs, rounded-up seconds, and model-specific audio pricing.
The payload builder now supports AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE in the delivery path and calls the shared audio Phase 2 fee helper.
The block verifier now supports AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE as a two-phase delivery precompile and validates delivery settlement fees with the same shared audio Phase 2 fee helper.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port for builder/verifier parity: both sides now price audio Phase 2 delivery settlements through the shared duration-based helper, so malformed duration extraction fails instead of falling back to generic byte-length pricing.

related_but_insufficient_code: Payload building passes delivery result length, not decoded audio duration, to calculate_async_fees_phase2.
Block verification extracts delivery result bytes and also passes result.len() to calculate_async_fees_phase2.
calculate_async_fees_phase2 has no AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE branch and no calculate_audio_fees_phase2 helper exists.

notes: Mapped after local parity fix. The catalog wording was corrected to current downstream outputDurationMs/rounded-seconds semantics.
rationale: The payload builder adds audio duration extraction from delivery_result slot 6 and uses calculate_audio_fees_phase2. Block verification mirrors this by extracting duration from settlement input and calculating the same audio fees, rejecting malformed inputs. Later downstream changes clarified that slot 6 stores milliseconds and pricing uses rounded-up seconds with model-specific audio pricing. The durable behavior is builder/verifier parity for duration-based audio Phase 2 fees rather than byte length or image dimensions.
statement: For audio Phase 2 delivery settlements, payload building and block verification must extract outputDurationMs from the delivery result, convert it to rounded-up pricing seconds, and calculate fees using calculate_audio_fees_phase2.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build an audio Phase 2 settlement whose delivery result encodes a known millisecond duration and assert payload settlement fees equal calculate_audio_fees_phase2 for the rounded pricing duration. Then verify a block containing that settlement succeeds, while a malformed or too-short delivery result causes block verification to reject it.
summary: Audio Phase 2 duration pricing is mapped in both builder and verifier paths.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Focused fee-crate tests cover calculate_audio_fees_phase2, and cargo check validates that both payload builder and block verifier use the shared audio delivery fee helper.
title: Audio Phase 2 uses duration pricing
